key_themes: Profit guidance beat and EBITDA growth in FY2026
Debenhams turnaround progress and double-digit growth targets
Ongoing revenue decline and retail sector pressures
Executive compensation scrutiny (high pay and potential £150m bonuses)
Neutral analyst consensus with noted valuation upside
summary: Boohoo Group (now closely tied to Debenhams with ticker DEBS.L, formerly BOO.L) reported a strong operational update in late March 2026, beating adjusted EBITDA guidance (up ~27-36% to £53m), lifting profit targets, and expressing confidence in its turnaround plan. This has driven mildly positive sentiment and solid YTD stock performance, though the company continues to grapple with significantly declining revenues, past losses, share dilution, and controversy over large potential CEO bonuses that have drawn criticism from investors like Frasers Group. Analysts remain neutral overall with an average price target implying upside, but technicals show near-term caution.[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/BOO/)[[2]](https://global.morningstar.com/en-gb/news/alliance-news/1774863545038805200/boohoo-confident-in-turnaround-plan-after-annual-earnings-growth)[[3]](https://www.investing.com/equities/boohoo)
sources: ~8-10 recent news articles (Yahoo Finance, Investing.com, LSE, Morningstar – focused on March 2026 earnings beat), Limited relevant X/investor posts (semantic and keyword searches returned <5 directly relevant investor discussions amid high noise/unrelated content)
